patterns_discovered.description: Uses multiple levels of fixtures for setup: configuration mocking, auth mocking, sample data objects, and API response mocking
Consistent use of @patch decorators to mock external dependencies (FulfillmentOutbound, FbaInventory, credentials)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Isolates unit tests from external API calls and dependencies

patterns_discovered.description: Tests grouped by functionality in separate test classes (TestMCFClientInitialization, TestCreateFulfillmentOrder, etc.)
patterns_discovered.organization_benefit: Clear separation of concerns and easy test navigation

patterns_discovered.description: Tests cover both API errors (response.errors) and exceptions (SellingApiException)
Mock responses structured to match actual API response format with payload and errors attributes

gotchas_discovered.description: Tests verify that marketplace_id is automatically set from client config if not provided in order object
gotchas_discovered.implication: API calls must validate and set marketplace_id when order object lacks it

gotchas_discovered.description: API can return errors in response.errors list or raise SellingApiException
gotchas_discovered.implication: Error handling must account for both response-level errors and exception-level errors

gotchas_discovered.description: Tests use both model objects and dictionary representations (to_sp_api_dict), suggesting bidirectional conversion needed
gotchas_discovered.implication: Models must support both object and dictionary serialization for API communication

gotchas_discovered.description: Tests explicitly verify pagination token extraction and continuation with next_token parameter
gotchas_discovered.implication: Pagination must be handled carefully to avoid infinite loops or missed results

gotchas_discovered.description: Tests for credential refresh suggest tokens can expire; refresh must occur before token expiration
gotchas_discovered.implication: Client must implement proactive token refresh or retry logic with refresh

objectives_completed: Created comprehensive unit test suite for MCFClient
Implemented 40+ test cases covering all major client methods
Set up proper mocking infrastructure for external dependencies
Tested error handling paths for API failures
Verified pagination and credential refresh functionality
test_coverage_areas: Client initialization with default and custom authentication
Fulfillment order creation, retrieval, and cancellation
Shipment tracking and package details retrieval
Inventory queries and SKU availability checks
API error handling and exception wrapping

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration tests that use actual Amazon SP-API test environment credentials to verify mock assumptions are accurate
recommendations.rationale: Unit tests with mocks may not catch API contract changes or version-specific behavior differences

recommendations.recommendation: Add parametrized tests for different marketplace IDs and regions to ensure client works across all supported Amazon regions
recommendations.rationale: Current tests use single marketplace; different regions may have different API behavior

recommendations.recommendation: Add docstrings to test methods explaining the scenario being tested and expected behavior
recommendations.rationale: Long test suite could benefit from brief scenario descriptions for maintainability

recommendations.recommendation: Add performance tests for pagination to verify handling of large result sets
recommendations.rationale: Pagination tests cover correctness but not performance with large datasets

recommendations.recommendation: Extract common mock setup logic into helper methods to reduce duplication across test classes
recommendations.rationale: Multiple test classes have similar mock setup patterns that could be consolidated
